speaker: PARIS
text: See, ho! Who is that there?

speaker: DEIPHOBUS
text: It is the Lord Aeneas.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Is the Prince there in person?—

speaker: AENEAS
text: Had I so good occasion to lie long

speaker: AENEAS
text: As you, Prince Paris, nothing but heavenly business

speaker: AENEAS
text: Should rob my bedmate of my company.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: That’s my mind too.—Good morrow, Lord Aeneas.

speaker: PARIS
text: A valiant Greek, Aeneas; take his hand.

speaker: PARIS
text: Witness the process of your speech, wherein

speaker: PARIS
text: You told how Diomed a whole week by days

speaker: PARIS
text: Did haunt you in the field.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Health to you, valiant sir,

speaker: AENEAS
text: During all question of the gentle truce;

speaker: AENEAS
text: But when I meet you armed, as black defiance

speaker: AENEAS
text: As heart can think or courage execute.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: The one and other Diomed embraces.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Our bloods are now in calm, and, so long, health;

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: But when contention and occasion meet,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: By Jove, I’ll play the hunter for thy life

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: With all my force, pursuit, and policy.

speaker: AENEAS
text: And thou shalt hunt a lion that will fly

speaker: AENEAS
text: With his face backward. In human gentleness,

speaker: AENEAS
text: Welcome to Troy. Now, by Anchises’ life,

speaker: AENEAS
text: Welcome indeed. By Venus’ hand I swear

speaker: AENEAS
text: No man alive can love in such a sort

speaker: AENEAS
text: The thing he means to kill more excellently.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: We sympathize. Jove, let Aeneas live,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: If to my sword his fate be not the glory,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: A thousand complete courses of the sun!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: But in mine emulous honor let him die

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: With every joint a wound and that tomorrow.

speaker: AENEAS
text: We know each other well.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: We do, and long to know each other worse.

speaker: PARIS
text: This is the most despiteful gentle greeting,

speaker: PARIS
text: The noblest hateful love, that e’er I heard of.

speaker: PARIS
text:
To
                 
                Aeneas
                .
                 
              What business, lord, so early?

speaker: AENEAS
text: I was sent for to the King, but why I know not.

speaker: PARIS
text: His purpose meets you. ’Twas to bring this Greek

speaker: PARIS
text: To Calchas’ house, and there to render him,

speaker: PARIS
text: For the enfreed Antenor, the fair Cressid.

speaker: PARIS
text: Let’s have your company, or, if you please,

speaker: PARIS
text:
Haste there before us.
                 
                (
                Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Aeneas
                .
                )
               I constantly believe—

speaker: PARIS
text: Or, rather, call my thought a certain knowledge—

speaker: PARIS
text: My brother Troilus lodges there tonight.

speaker: PARIS
text: Rouse him, and give him note of our approach,

speaker: PARIS
text: With the whole quality whereof. I fear

speaker: PARIS
text: We shall be much unwelcome.

speaker: AENEAS
text: That I assure you.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Troilus had rather Troy were borne to Greece

speaker: AENEAS
text: Than Cressid borne from Troy.

speaker: PARIS
text: There is no help.

speaker: PARIS
text: The bitter disposition of the time

speaker: PARIS
text: Will have it so.—On, lord, we’ll follow you.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Good morrow, all.

speaker: PARIS
text: And tell me, noble Diomed, faith, tell me true,

speaker: PARIS
text: Even in the soul of sound good-fellowship,

speaker: PARIS
text: Who, in your thoughts, deserves fair Helen best,

speaker: PARIS
text: Myself or Menelaus?

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Both alike.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: He merits well to have her that doth seek her,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Not making any scruple of her soilure,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: With such a hell of pain and world of charge;

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: And you as well to keep her that defend her,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Not palating the taste of her dishonor,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: With such a costly loss of wealth and friends.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: He, like a puling cuckold, would drink up

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: The lees and dregs of a flat tamèd piece;

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: You, like a lecher, out of whorish loins

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Are pleased to breed out your inheritors.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Both merits poised, each weighs nor less nor more;

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: But he as he, the heavier for a whore.

speaker: PARIS
text: You are too bitter to your countrywoman.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: She’s bitter to her country. Hear me, Paris:

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: For every false drop in her bawdy veins

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: A Grecian’s life hath sunk; for every scruple

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Of her contaminated carrion weight

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: A Trojan hath been slain. Since she could speak,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: She hath not given so many good words breath

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: As for her Greeks and Trojans suffered death.

speaker: PARIS
text: Fair Diomed, you do as chapmen do,

speaker: PARIS
text: Dispraise the thing that they desire to buy.

speaker: PARIS
text: But we in silence hold this virtue well:

speaker: PARIS
text: We’ll not commend that not intend to sell.

speaker: PARIS
text: Here lies our way.
